questions.question: Which of the following roles can add new members to a Notion workspace but cannot change workspace-wide settings?

options.text: Workspace owner
options.explanation: Workspace owners can both add members and change workspace settings.

options.text: Membership admin (Enterprise plans only)
options.explanation: Membership admins can add members and edit permission groups, but they cannot change workspace settings.

options.text: Member
options.explanation: Members can create and share pages but cannot add new members.

options.text: Guest
options.explanation: Guests are external collaborators and cannot add members.

questions.question: If you want anyone on the internet to view a Notion page without needing a Notion account, which sharing method should you use?

options.text: Invite them as a guest
options.explanation: Guests still need a Notion account and are limited to the pages you invite them to.

options.text: Copy and send the page link
options.explanation: A plain link only works for people who already have access to the workspace.

options.text: Publish the page to the web
options.explanation: Publishing makes the page publicly accessible, optionally searchable, without requiring a Notion login.

options.text: Share the page with the General teamspace
options.explanation: That shares it only with members of the workspace, not the public.

questions.question: Which permission level allows a user to edit a page **and** share it with others?

options.text: Can edit
options.explanation: Can edit lets the user modify the page but not share it further.

options.text: Full access
options.explanation: Full access includes editing rights plus the ability to share the page with additional people.

options.text: Can comment
options.explanation: Can comment only allows leaving comments; no editing or sharing.

options.text: Can view
options.explanation: Can view provides read‑only access.

questions.question: A workspace owner wants to prevent members from publishing pages to the web. Which setting should they adjust?

options.text: Toggle off "Allow members to add guests" in the Security tab
options.explanation: That setting controls guest invitations, not web publishing.

options.text: Toggle off "Allow members to share publicly" in the Security tab
options.explanation: Disabling this option stops members from publishing pages to the web.

options.text: Remove the "Publish to web" button from the Share menu
options.explanation: The button cannot be manually removed; it’s controlled by security settings.

options.text: Delete the "General" teamspace
options.explanation: Teamspaces affect organization, not publishing permissions.

questions.question: Which of the following statements best describes the purpose of the "Allowed email domains" setting?

options.text: It restricts which external guests can be invited to a page.
options.explanation: Allowed email domains apply to workspace auto‑join, not guest invitations.

options.text: It automatically grants workspace access to anyone who signs up with an email from those domains.
options.explanation: When a user signs in with an allowed domain, they are offered the option to join the workspace automatically.

options.text: It determines which domains can be used in page URLs when publishing to the web.
options.explanation: Domain settings don’t affect public URLs; they affect workspace membership.

options.text: It blocks all emails from unapproved domains from receiving page links.
options.explanation: The setting doesn’t block link sharing; it only controls auto‑join eligibility.

questions.question: Your design team needs to collaborate on a project spec, but you don’t want the whole company to edit it. Which sharing approach is most appropriate?

options.text: Share the page with the General teamspace and give everyone "Can view" access.
options.explanation: While this limits editing, it also makes the page visible to the entire company, which may be unnecessary.

options.text: Create a permission group for the design team and share the page with that group, granting "Can edit".
options.explanation: Permission groups let you share with the entire design team while restricting access for others.

options.text: Publish the page to the web with "Can comment" enabled.
options.explanation: Publishing makes the page publicly accessible, which is not needed for internal design work.

options.text: Invite each designer individually as a guest.
options.explanation: Guests are for external collaborators; designers are workspace members.

questions.question: Which permission level should you assign to HR managers for a company‑wide benefits page if you want all employees to see the page but only HR to make changes?

options.text: Can view for everyone, Full access for HR managers
options.explanation: Everyone can view, while HR managers have full editing and sharing rights.

options.text: Can edit for everyone, Can comment for HR managers
options.explanation: This would allow all employees to edit, which is not desired.

options.text: Can comment for everyone, Can view for HR managers
options.explanation: HR would not be able to edit the page.

options.text: Full access for everyone, Can view for HR managers
options.explanation: Everyone would be able to edit and share, which is too permissive.

questions.question: An Enterprise workspace owner wants to stop members from moving pages to other workspaces. Which toggle should they disable?

options.text: "Allow members to export content"
options.explanation: Exporting is unrelated to moving pages between workspaces.

options.text: "Allow members to add guests"
options.explanation: That controls guest invitations, not page movement.

options.text: "Allow members to move pages to other workspaces"
options.explanation: Disabling this specific toggle prevents members from moving pages across workspaces.

options.text: "Allow members to share publicly"
options.explanation: That restricts public sharing, not internal page relocation.

questions.question: When you publish a Notion page to the web, which of the following options can you configure for public users?

options.text: Only "Can view" – public users can never comment or edit.
options.explanation: You can also allow comment or edit, depending on your settings.

options.text: Choose between "Can view", "Can comment", or "Can edit" for the public link.
options.explanation: The "Show link options" dropdown lets you set view, comment, or edit permissions for anyone with the link.

options.text: Set a password that must be entered before viewing.
options.explanation: Notion does not currently support password‑protected public pages.

options.text: Limit access to specific email domains even when published.
options.explanation: Public publishing bypasses domain restrictions; it’s open to anyone with the link.

questions.question: Which of the following best explains why you might use the "General" teamspace to share a page?

options.text: It restricts the page to a single department only.
options.explanation: The General teamspace includes everyone, not a single department.

options.text: It automatically gives the page "Full access" to all members.
options.explanation: Permissions still need to be set; the teamspace only defines the audience.

options.text: It provides a quick way to share a page with every member of the company.
options.explanation: Dragging a page into the General teamspace shares it with the entire organization.

options.text: It makes the page publicly searchable on the web.
options.explanation: Public searchability requires publishing to the web, not sharing via a teamspace.
